parsedContent.instructions: Please read all instructions carefully before you begin. Write your full name and index number clearly on the answer booklet. Do NOT open this paper until instructed to do so. Use only a BLUE or BLACK pen for answers, and a PENCIL for drawings and graphs. Calculators and mathematical instruments may be used. Show ALL working clearly for calculation questions; unsupported answers will not receive full credit. Diagrams must be neatly drawn, labeled, and titled where applicable. Ensure each answer is clearly numbered to match the question. Only your final answer will be marked; neat crossing out is allowed.

sections.name: A
sections.description: Multiple Choice, True/False, and Fill-in-the-Blank Questions

questions.text: Fill in the blanks using the most appropriate term from the box below. Each term may be used once or not at all.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: A ................................ is a magnet made from a coil of wire carrying an electric current wound around an iron core.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: A ................................ is a cylindrical coil of wire that acts as a magnet when electric current flows through it.

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.text: When a conductor moves through a magnetic field, a(n) ................................ is produced in the conductor.

questions.text: Choose the letter of the best answer for each question.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Which of the following best describes the term 'noise' in a communication system?

options.text: The loudness of the transmitted signal
Any unwanted disturbance that interferes with the transmitted signal
The frequency at which the signal is transmitted
The distance between the transmitter and receiver

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: Which electronic component allows current to flow in only one direction?

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.text: In a transistor used as a switch, the transistor is said to be in saturation when it is:

subQuestions.label: d)
subQuestions.text: Which type of wave is used in mobile phone communication?

questions.text: Refraction is the bending of light as it passes from one medium to another.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: State Snell's Law of refraction and write its mathematical expression.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Snell's Law: n₁ sinθ₁ = n₂ sinθ₂

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: A ray of light travels from air into a glass block. The angle of incidence is 45° and the refractive index of the glass is 1.5. Calculate the angle of refraction.

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.text: Draw a clearly labelled ray diagram showing the refraction of light as it passes from air into a denser glass block. Include the normal line, angles of incidence and refraction.

subQuestions.label: d)
subQuestions.text: Define the term 'critical angle' and state the condition under which total internal reflection occurs.
subQuestions.correctAnswer: Critical angle: the angle of incidence in the denser medium at which the refracted ray travels along the interface

questions.text: A student investigates how the extension of a spring depends on the load applied to it.

subQuestions.label: a)
subQuestions.text: Complete the third column by converting each extension value from centimetres to metres.

subQuestions.label: b)
subQuestions.text: State the law that describes the relationship between force and extension for a spring within its elastic limit.

subQuestions.label: c)
subQuestions.text: Plot a graph of Load (y-axis) against Extension in metres (x-axis) on the provided graph paper. Draw the best-fit straight line.

subQuestions.label: d)
subQuestions.text: i) From your graph, determine the slope (gradient) of the line and state its physical meaning. ii) Calculate the spring constant k, including its unit.

subQuestions.label: e)
subQuestions.text: If a load of 3.5 N is applied to the spring, predict the extension using your graph or formula.
